channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Chelsea sign Rogers in record-breaking £117m deal
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Chelsea have completed the signing of Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa in a record-breaking £117m deal.

item.title: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far — Pentagon chief
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Iran war has cost the United States $37.5 billion to date, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth revealed. Get the latest figures on the Iran war cost.
Read More: https://punchng.com/iran-war-has-cost-us-37-5bn-so-far-pentagon-chief/

item.title: Reps receive constitution amendment report on state police bill
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives has received the constitution amendment report on the State Police Bill, moving Nigeria closer to decentralised policing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-receive-constitution-amendment-report-on-state-police-bill/

item.title: Enzo Fernandez: Argentina played with 'pride' and 'humility'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Enzo Fernandez has praised Argentina for playing with "pride" and "humility" at the 2026 World Cup after it was confirmed FIFA will investigate the team's behaviour after the final.

item.title: Nigeria’s external reserves rise to $52.52bn, says CBN gov
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
CBN Governor Yemi Cardoso announces Nigeria’s external reserves have risen to $52.52bn, sufficient for 11 months of imports.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerias-external-reserves-rise-to-52-52bn-says-cbn-gov/

item.dc:creator: Completesports

Santos, who managed Bernardo Silva at the international level, spoke highly of the player’s abili

item.title: This ETF Tax Loophole Is All the Rage Among Rich Americans
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A bespoke fund is becoming a must-have tool for wealthy investors looking to defer — and sometimes even avoid — capital gains tax.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kogi State Governor, Usman Ododo, has said that he neither plays with nor negotiates with bandits in the state. item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: Africa now world’s hunger capital, as global hunger falls for third year
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Global hunger declined for the third consecutive year in 2025, but Africa has emerged as the epicentre of the world's food crisis, with the continent now accounting for the highest number of hungry people globally, a new United Nations report has revealed. The post Africa now world’s hunger ca

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its own pre-release models
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.description: The Naira, which has remained relatively stable due to sustained reforms, also recorded a slight gain on Monday. The post Naira gains N4.80 at official market appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Moody’s Lifts Argentina Credit Rating in Fresh Boost to Milei
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Moody’s Ratings upgraded Argentina’s credit score, giving the nation its third sovereign upgrade in less than three months and validating President Javier Milei’s economic overhaul plan.

item.title: Alaska Airlines Narrowly Beats Estimates as Fuel Costs Stay High
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Alaska Air Group Inc. earnings outlook slightly beat analyst predictions as the airline grapples with rising fuel prices from the war in the Middle East.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Thirty-one companies have emerged as winners of 37 oil and gas blocks in the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC)'s 2025 Licensing Round. The post NUPRC oil block auction: 31 firms emerge winners as 13 assets receive no bids appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: AEDC begins repairs after resolving Maitama substation crisis
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
AEDC has commenced repairs on the damaged C2 Maitama Substation, restoring power to affected areas after resolving invasion issues. Learn more about the AE
Read More: https://punchng.com/aedc-begins-repairs-after-resolving-maitama-substation-crisis/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Reps warn agencies shunning summons
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives issues a final warning to agencies shunning summons in the PFIPC scandal probe, threatening constitutional powers to compel ap
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-reps-warn-agencies-shunning-summons/

item.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after days of unrest over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

item.title: NBA Election: I was disenfranchised like many lawyers, still Badejo-Okusanya could’ve won – SAN
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “An electoral process that technically disenfranchises a significant number of eligible voters cannot, by any stretch of the imagination, be described as wholly free, fair, credible and inclusive,” the SAN and former national officer of the NBA said. The post NBA Election: I was disenfranchised like

item.title: Anambra Begins Dredging Ikenga Waterway To Tackle Flooding In Awka
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The waterway, which drains into the Ezu River through Agu Awka in Awka South Local Government Area, is being reopened to improve drainage and reduce flooding in the state capital. The post Anambra Begins Dredging Ikenga Waterway To Tackle Flooding In Awka appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Stop Campaign Of Calumny Against INEC, Arewa Youth Forum Tells Opposition
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Arewa Youth Forum (AYF) has urged opposition parties to stop what it described as a campaign of calumny against the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), expressing confidence in the leadership of the Commission’s Chairman, Prof. Joash Amupitan (SAN).   The f

item.title: Outgoing British High Commissioner eulogises Tinubu’s economic reforms
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Outgoing British High Commissioner to Nigeria, Richard Montgomery, on Tuesday said the economic reforms of President Bola Tinubu would put Nigeria on a better footing in the long term. The post Outgoing British High Commissioner eulogises Tinubu’s economic reforms appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: FG Moves To Reduce Cargo Clearance From 21 To Seven Days At Nigerian Ports
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: .Dismantles illegal checkpoints along Apapa and Tin Can island ports corridor Deji Elumoye in Abuja The Federal Government has hinted of plans to reduce cargo clearance at all Nigerian ports

item.title: The economy Nigeria isn’t governing
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria has two economies. Only one is governed. One appears in GDP figures, tax records and government policy documents. The read more The economy Nigeria isn’t governing

item.title: EFCC Arrests Abuja Realtor Omokamo After Court Order
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arrested Abuja-based realtor, Rebecca Omokamo Godwin-Isaac, after a Federal High Court in Abuja ordered the anti-graft agency to take her into custody and produce her for the continuation of her trial over alleged land fraud, forgery and money

item.title: FG accepts final AGROW report, clears way for $500m World Bank programme to move to implementation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has accepted the final report of the National Technical Working Group (NTWG) for the World Bank-supported US$500 million Sustainable Agricultural Value Chains for Growth Programme (AGROW). The post FG accepts final AGROW report, clears way for $500m World Bank programme to mov
